title: L5: Large Quantities
description: Converting between simple units of representation size and describing physical ways bits are represented.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick check from Numbers in Binary.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-dna',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 1010 to denary.","answer":"10","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 5 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"0101","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"What are the 4-bit place values from left to right?","answers":["8 4 2 1","8,4,2,1","8, 4, 2, 1"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Representation Size
steps.content:
Large quantities

Bits are very small, so larger files are measured using larger units. Use these facts first, then choose whether to multiply or divide.

FactHow to use it
1 nibble = 4 bitsNibbles to bits: multiply by 4
1 byte = 8 bitsBytes to bits: multiply by 8. Bits to bytes: divide by 8
1 KB = 1000 bytesKB to bytes: multiply by 1000
1 MB = 1000 KBMB to KB: multiply by 1000

A real device also needs a physical way to represent 0 and 1, such as low/high voltage, no light/light, or no charge/charge.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-intro-check',questions:[{"prompt":"How many bits are in 1 byte?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bytes are in 1 KB in this course?","answer":"1000","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Key Ideas: Multiply or Divide
steps.content:
Key ideas

Unit conversion is easier if you decide the direction first. Going from a bigger unit to a smaller unit means multiply. Going from a smaller unit to a bigger unit means divide.

ConversionDirectionOperation
bytes to bitsbigger to smallermultiply by 8
bits to bytessmaller to biggerdivide by 8
KB to bytesbigger to smallermultiply by 1000
bytes to KBsmaller to biggerdivide by 1000

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-vocab-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Bytes to bits: A = multiply by 8, B = divide by 8. Type A or B.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Bits to bytes: A = multiply by 8, B = divide by 8. Type A or B.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Units
steps.content:
Step through unit conversion

Click through two common representation size conversions.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr5-stepper-1',steps:[{"title":"Convert 3 bytes to bits","html":"

1 byte = 8 bits

","note":"Start with the conversion fact."},{"title":"Multiply by 8","html":"

3 bytes = 3 x 8 bits = 24 bits

","note":"Bytes to bits means multiply by 8."},{"title":"Convert 2 KB to bytes","html":"

1 KB = 1000 bytes

2 KB = 2 x 1000 bytes = 2000 bytes

","note":"In this course, we use 1000 bytes in a kilobyte."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-guided1-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 3 bytes to bits.","answer":"24","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 2 KB to bytes.","answer":"2000","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Decide the Operation
steps.content:
Step through the operation choice

Click through how to choose multiply or divide before calculating.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr5-stepper-3',steps:[{"title":"Convert 6 bytes to bits","html":"

bytes to bits

","note":"Bits are smaller than bytes, so we are going bigger to smaller."},{"title":"Multiply by 8","html":"

6 x 8 = 48 bits

","note":"One byte has 8 bits."},{"title":"Convert 4000 bytes to KB","html":"

bytes to KB

","note":"KB is bigger than bytes, so we are going smaller to bigger."},{"title":"Divide by 1000","html":"

4000 / 1000 = 4 KB

","note":"In this course, 1 KB = 1000 bytes."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-guided3-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 6 bytes to bits.","answer":"48","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 4000 bytes to KB.","answer":"4","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Mini Check: Operation First
steps.content:
Mini check: operation first

Do not calculate yet. Choose the operation. Type multiply or divide.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-mini-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Bytes to bits: multiply or divide?","answers":["multiply","x","times"],"right":"Correct: multiply by 8."},{"prompt":"Bits to bytes: multiply or divide?","answers":["divide","/"],"right":"Correct: divide by 8."},{"prompt":"KB to bytes: multiply or divide?","answers":["multiply","x","times"],"right":"Correct: multiply by 1000."},{"prompt":"Bytes to KB: multiply or divide?","answers":["divide","/"],"right":"Correct: divide by 1000."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Physical Bits
steps.content:
Step through physical representation

A bit is a 0 or 1, but real devices need a physical way to store or carry that 0 or 1.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr5-stepper-2',steps:[{"title":"The abstract bit","html":"
1
","note":"This is the representation idea: one binary digit."},{"title":"One possible physical form","html":"
Device0 could be1 could be
WireLow voltageHigh voltage
Optical fibreNo light pulseLight pulse
StorageNo stored chargeStored charge
","note":"The exact physical form depends on the device."},{"title":"Same bit, different media","html":"

0 and 1 are ideas. Devices represent them physically.

","note":"This links back to symbols being carried on physical media."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-guided2-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = voltage, B = lesson title, C = paragraph. Which can physically represent bits?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = light pulse, B = punctuation mark, C = table row. Which can carry bits in fibre?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: convert units

Use the rule in the question. Type only the number.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-practice-a',questions:[{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 2 bytes to bits.","answer":"16","right":"Correct: 2 x 8 = 16."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 5 bytes to bits.","answer":"40","right":"Correct: 5 x 8 = 40."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 24 bits to bytes.","answer":"3","right":"Correct: 24 / 8 = 3."},{"prompt":"1 KB = 1000 bytes. Convert 4 KB to bytes.","answer":"4000","right":"Correct: 4 x 1000 = 4000."},{"prompt":"1 nibble = 4 bits. Convert 3 nibbles to bits.","answer":"12","right":"Correct: 3 x 4 = 12."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: physical bits

Use the options. Type A, B or C.

LetterPhysical representation
ALow voltage / high voltage in a wire
BNo light pulse / light pulse in fibre
CNo stored charge / stored charge in memory
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-practice-b',questions:[{"prompt":"Which option represents bits using voltage in a wire?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Which option represents bits using light pulses?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Which option represents bits using stored charge?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A bit is A = an idea represented physically, B = always a printed number, C = always a sound. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Which option would be used in fibre optic cable?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Extension: Larger Unit Practice
steps.content:
Extension challenge

Fast finishers can try these extra conversions. The rule is shown in each question.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-extension',questions:[{"prompt":"1 MB = 1000 KB. Convert 2 MB to KB.","answer":"2000","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 KB = 1000 bytes. Convert 8000 bytes to KB.","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 10 bytes to bits.","answer":"80","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 64 bits to bytes.","answer":"8","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 generated unit and physical representation questions correctly to complete this step.
steps.js: (function(){var bank=[{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 2 bytes to bits.","answer":"16","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 32 bits to bytes.","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 7 bytes to bits.","answer":"56","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 KB = 1000 bytes. Convert 3 KB to bytes.","answer":"3000","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in a nibble?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in a byte?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = voltage, B = lesson title, C = paragraph. Which can physically represent bits?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}];var qs=[];for(var i=0;i<5;i++){qs.push(bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)]);}BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-endless',questions:qs});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Answer all questions.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr5-exam',questions:[{"prompt":"How many bits are in one byte?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 6 bytes to bits.","answer":"48","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 KB = 1000 bytes. Convert 5 KB to bytes.","answer":"5000","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 16 bits to bytes.","answer":"2","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = low/high voltage, B = a file name, C = a lesson order. Which can physically represent binary digits?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}]});

quizQuestions.q: How many bits are in one byte?
How many bits are in one nibble?
Which could physically represent a 0 or 1 in a wire?
Which could physically carry bits through fibre optic cable?
